title: Hlidskjalf Datagram
description: Event datagram for the Hlidskjalf messaging protocol. The envelope is self-describing: kind identifies what the datagram IS (content type), source identifies who SENT it (the emitting binary).

timestamp.description: Unix epoch timestamp in seconds (float64). When the datagram was created, not received.

source.description: The binary that emitted this datagram via datagram (e.g. "syn", "bifrost", "lockfile_monitor", "send_alert").

kind.description: Content type — what this datagram IS. Drives rendering (which component draws it) and filtering (which chip toggles it).

classifier.description: Sub-classification within the kind. Closed enum per kind, required for kinds with significant sub-types (traffic, quality). Absent for simple kinds.

priority.description: Severity level for threshold filtering and alert modality activation.

workspace.description: Project/workspace name (e.g. "yggdrasil", "bragi"). Empty string if not workspace-scoped.

detail.description: Human-readable one-line summary. Primary text for simple kinds (alert, canary, notify). May be absent when the payload speaks for itself.

speech.description: Voice alert text composed for spoken delivery. Triggers TTS when priority exceeds user's speech threshold.

payload.description: Structured data whose shape is determined by kind. quality: payload.quality.schema.json. traffic: payload.traffic.schema.json. Simple kinds (alert, canary, notify) have no payload.

message.description: Representative issue message (from the first issue in the group).

severity.description: Issue severity within the quality report.

count.description: Total number of issues in this group.

file_count.description: Number of unique files containing this issue.

signal.description: Architectural signal — what was detected. Empty string when not applicable.

direction.description: Suggested fix direction. Empty string when not applicable.

canary.description: Context loss canary token. Empty string when not applicable.

locations.description: File-aggregated locations. Issues in the same file collapse into one entry.

quality_location.description: Single-line: { file, line }. Multi-line: { file, lines }. Exactly one of line or lines must be present.

file.description: File path relative to workspace root.

line.description: Line number (when file has exactly one issue in this group).

lines.description: Line numbers (when file has multiple issues in this group).

total.description: Sum of all issue counts across all groups.

groups.description: One entry per unique (tool, code) combination.

traffic_tool_input.description: The raw input object passed to a Claude Code tool invocation. Shape varies per tool.

traffic_kind.description: Classification of the exchange. startup: session start/resume/post-compaction. conversation: user interaction. tool: non-Task tool use. subagent: Task tool dispatch. planning: EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode.

system_injection.description: True when the exchange contains platform-injected system-reminders (not user-generated hooks). User hooks (syn_report, PostToolUse, etc.) are filtered out. Only Anthropic's arbitrary injections set this flag.

source.description: Reference to the raw exchange in mainexch JSONL. Format: filename:line_number. Clicking navigates to the exact raw API exchange.

user.description: User-authored text content. Multiple text blocks concatenated with blank line separator.

assistant.description: Assistant-authored text content (visible response). Multiple text blocks concatenated with blank line separator.

thinking.description: Assistant's chain-of-thought reasoning. Multiple thinking blocks concatenated with blank line separator.

system.description: System-reminder text blocks injected into user messages. Includes both user-generated hooks and platform injections. Multiple reminders concatenated with blank line separator.

tool_return.description: Tool result content. All tool_result blocks regardless of which tool produced them. Multiple results concatenated with blank line separator.

shell.description: Bash tool invocation(s). Single object if one call, array if multiple.

tool_use.description: Generic tool invocation(s) for unmapped tool names (TaskUpdate, TaskOutput, LSP, etc.).

instructions.description: New system prompt blocks (CLAUDE.md additions, workspace instructions). Text content of diff_system_blocks, concatenated with blank line separator.

tools_added.description: Names of newly appeared tool definitions (e.g. when MCP tools load or session tools change).
